title: Agentic AI in Supply Chain
summary: Autonomous agents for resilient and intelligent supply chains.
description: A specialised course on designing, evaluating and governing AI agents across planning, procurement, logistics, inventory and supply-chain control processes.
audience: Supply-chain, operations and engineering management students and professionals exploring intelligent workflow automation.
prerequisites: Introductory knowledge of supply-chain processes; prior AI experience is not required.
outcomes: Map supply-chain decisions suitable for AI-agent support.
Design agent roles, tools, data access and human controls.
Evaluate reliability, coordination and operational risk.
Develop performance measures for agent-enabled workflows.
Propose a phased implementation for a supply-chain use case.

modules.title: Supply-chain decisions and agent opportunities
modules.description: Planning, procurement, inventory, logistics and service.

modules.title: Agent design and workflow orchestration
modules.description: Roles, tools, memory, hand-offs and escalation.

modules.title: Data and enterprise-system integration
modules.description: Operational data, APIs and process constraints.

modules.title: Reliability, controls and governance
modules.description: Human oversight, exception handling and accountability.

modules.title: Pilot design and scale-up
modules.description: Metrics, operating change and implementation roadmap.
